perturbation.original_text: 1.3 “ Subsidiary ” of any entity means any other entity in which such first entity owns or Controls, directly or indirectly, an amount of the voting securities, other voting interests or voting partnership interests sufficient to elect at least a majority of such other entity’s board of directors or other governing body (or, if there are no such voting interests, 50% or more of the equity interests of such other entity). For purposes of this Section 1.3 , “Control” means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership or limited liability company, whether through the ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 “ Subsidiary ” of any entity means any other entity in which such first entity owns or Controls, directly or indirectly, at least 75% of the voting securities, other voting interests or voting partnership interests and only if the entity is incorporated in the European Union. For purposes of this Section 1.3 , “Control” means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an individual, corporation, partnership, limited partnership or limited liability company, but only through the ownership of voting securities.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Subsidiary' requires owning a majority of voting securities. The modified definition requires owning at least 75% and being incorporated in the European Union. This creates a contradiction because sections relying on the original definition, such as those dealing with the CEDC Group's obligations, would now exclude entities that are majority-owned but not 75% owned or not EU-incorporated, creating confusion about the scope of those obligations. The definition of control is limited to only being possible with ownership of voting securities, therefore making the original sentence that mentions 'by contract or otherwise' non-applicable now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information pertaining to the business, operations, brands, marketing plans, financial matters, legal matters, products, projects, business plans or practices, research and development, product development, intellectual property, financial models, trademarks, trade secrets, accounting and financing data, and methods of production, distribution or procurement, suppliers, distributors, consultants, advisors or employees, directors or officers of CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries (defined below) (together, the “ CEDC Group ”) that is disclosed or otherwise made available, either orally or in writing, by any member of the CEDC Group to the Recipient or its affiliates, agents, or advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, attorneys, banks and other sources of equity and debt financing and accountants) (collectively, “ Representatives ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Subsidiary' based on a majority ownership of voting securities, while the changed text requires at least 75% ownership and incorporation in the EU. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (Section 1.1), which refers to 'CEDC or any of its Subsidiaries' and their 'Confidential Information'. The change narrows the definition of Subsidiary, so Section 1.1 now excludes subsidiaries that were previously included, creating a contradiction about which entities are covered under the confidentiality agreement. Furthermore, the modification limits the definition of 'Control' to only being possible through ownership of voting securities, thus contradicting the original 'by contract or otherwise' clause, creating further ambiguity regarding the scope of control, therefore this falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information, as defined in Section 1.1, excluding information orally disclosed, will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever, unless required by a valid subpoena. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable efforts, but not necessarily the strictest, care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: The original section mandates 'strictest confidence.' The modified version now permits disclosure if required by a subpoena and changes 'strictest care' to 'reasonable efforts.' This introduces a contradiction: other sections might assume an absolute obligation of confidentiality, but this section allows for exceptions. The first sentence makes a contradictory statement excluding orally disclosed information, while the previous 1.1 definition includes it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 Subject to Section 1.2 below, “ Confidential Information ” means only written confidential, secret, proprietary or other non-public information directly pertaining to the restructuring plans of CEDC, excluding any information related to its day-to-day business operations, marketing plans, or financial matters outside the scope of the Restructuring. This includes only documents explicitly labeled as 'Restructuring Confidential' and excludes all other forms of communication or data. This definition applies solely for the purposes of Section 2 of this agreement. Furthermore, it only covers documentation disclosed after February 18, 2013. Any earlier documents are excluded from the definition of confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it excludes orally disclosed information from the definition of Confidential Information, conflicting with the original and general understanding of what constitutes confidential information as initially defined. Furthermore, the introduction of 'unless required by a valid subpoena' directly contradicts the requirement of 'strictest confidence' as an absolute obligation, leading to uncertainty about the level of confidentiality expected. The Contradicted Text also includes the caveat that 'Confidential Information' means only written confidential information, but the Changed Text contradicts this by only excluding orally disclosed information.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 The Recipient shall ensure that each of its Representatives who are either provided with Confidential Information, or otherwise have access to such Confidential Information, are informed of its confidential nature and are directed to abide by the terms of this Agreement applicable to Representatives or appropriate duties or obligations of confidentiality imposing confidentiality obligations on such Representatives (except that there shall be no requirement to so inform where the Representative to whom the information is to be disclosed is subject to professional obligations to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information or is otherwise bound by requirements of confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information). The Recipient agrees to be liable for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its affiliates or Representatives. The Recipient shall use reasonable efforts to provide written notice to CEDC of any use or disclosure of Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement of which Recipient is aware, including without limitation written details regarding the circumstances and the identity of the individuals or entities who as a result received or may have received access to such Confidential Information. - 2 -
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 The Recipient shall ensure that only legal Representatives who are directly involved in the Restructuring and are pre-approved in writing by CEDC are provided with Confidential Information. These Representatives must sign individual confidentiality agreements directly with CEDC. The Recipient is NOT liable for any breach of this Agreement by its Representatives, unless the breach was a direct result of intentional misconduct by the Recipient itself. The Recipient shall only be required to provide notice to CEDC of any use or disclosure of Confidential Information in breach of this Agreement if the Recipient is legally compelled to do so by a court order.
perturbation.explanation: The original section holds the Recipient liable for breaches by its Representatives. The modified version removes that liability unless there's intentional misconduct by the Recipient. It also creates internal contradiction: Originally, the Recipient must take efforts in giving notice to CEDC of any use or disclosure of confidential information. However, with the changes, the Recipient is NOT liable for any breach of the agreement by it's representatives, unless it was a direct result of intentional misconduct by the Recipient itself. The Recipient also is only required to provide notice to CEDC by court order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The Recipient agrees that it will not, without the prior written consent of CEDC, directly or indirectly, disclose all or any portion of the Confidential Information, or the substance thereof, to any third party other than its Representatives in connection with the Purpose, except, subject to Section 2.3 below, to the extent required by applicable law or legal process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating the Recipient is NOT liable for breaches by its Representatives unless there's intentional misconduct by the Recipient. The original text, and the contradicted text (2.2), implies that the Recipient is responsible for its Representatives and their actions regarding confidential information. The changed text restricts liability and notice requirements, which directly conflicts with the original broader responsibility for Representative's actions and notice obligations, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Recipient agrees that, upon written request by CEDC, all Confidential Information (and all copies, summaries and notes of the contents or parts thereof) and all Company property received by the Recipient shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, be either returned to CEDC or, to the extent technically practicable, destroyed (at the Recipient’s or its Representatives’ option), except that Recipient and its Representatives may retain copies of Confidential Information as is required to comply with applicable law or regulation or professional standards or internal compliance requirements. The Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 hereof shall survive the return of such tangible embodiments of Confidential Information until termination of this Agreement. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, neither Recipient nor any of its Representatives shall be required to delete electronically stored Confidential Information to the extent such deletion would be technologically impracticable or inconsistent with the archival records retention policy of the Recipient or its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Recipient agrees that, upon a unanimous vote of CEDC's board of directors, all written Confidential Information (excluding any summaries or notes created by the Recipient) received after the date of this agreement shall, within 180 days, be returned to CEDC. Destruction is no longer an acceptable alternative. The Recipient and its Representatives may retain only a single archival copy for legal purposes, but must render it completely inaccessible and unusable. The Recipient’s obligations under Section 2 hereof do not survive the return of such tangible embodiments of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, Recipient and its Representatives shall be required to delete all electronically stored Confidential Information and provide written certification of such deletion to CEDC within 30 days of the request.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Recipient has the option to return or destroy Confidential Information. The modified section removes the destruction option and only allows returning the materials and within 180 days. Originally, the Recipient's obligations under Section 2 survive the return of the confidential information. The modified section however, they don't survive. Now there is a direct contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 The Recipient agrees that all Confidential Information, as defined in Section 1.1, excluding information orally disclosed, will be held and maintained by it in the strictest confidence, will be used by it solely and exclusively for the purpose of evaluating, negotiating and implementing a Restructuring, and will not, directly or indirectly, be used or disclosed by it for any other purpose whatsoever, unless required by a valid subpoena. CEDC acknowledges that Recipient is engaged in businesses similar to those conducted by CEDC and the CEDC Group or other industries in the ordinary course of Recipient’s business. The Recipient will use reasonable efforts, but not necessarily the strictest, care to maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information, provided that such care shall be at least as great as the precautions taken by the Recipient to protect its own confidential information of similar nature.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it modifies the original terms of confidential information handling. Originally, the Recipient's obligations under Section 2 survived the return of the confidential information, but in the modified section, they don't. Additionally, the changed text mandates the deletion of all electronically stored Confidential Information, contradicting the original text which allowed for exceptions based on technological impracticability or archival policies. This conflicts with Section 2.1 which states that the Recipient will maintain the confidentiality of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Termination. 7.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect at all times until the earliest to occur of (i) the filing of an exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement with respect to the 2016 Notes or Convertible Notes, provided that , to the extent CEDC and the Recipient continue discussions on the terms of a Restructuring following such filing, this period shall extend until the filing of an amended exchange offer, offering memorandum or disclosure statement reflecting such discussions or (ii) 9:00 a.m. (GMT) on March 5, 2013, whereupon this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect (the “ Termination Date ”), other than with respect to the rights and obligations of CEDC and Recipient per Section 7.2 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Termination. 7.1 The provisions of this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect at all times until the completion of the Restructuring. This agreement will never terminate unless the restructuring is completed. In the event that there is no restructuring to be done, this agreement is not enforceable. The clause concerning 9:00 a.m. (GMT) on March 5, 2013, whereupon this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force or effect (the “ Termination Date ”), other than with respect to the rights and obligations of CEDC and Recipient per Section 7.2 hereof is null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement has a defined termination date. The modified agreement states that the agreement terminates only upon the completion of the Restructuring, creating a contradiction. The modified version then states in the event there is no restructuring to be done, this agreement is not enforceable which contradicts the rest of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Remedies . The Recipient acknowledges that a breach of any of the terms of this Agreement may cause irreparable harm to CEDC for which CEDC may not be adequately compensated by money damages. Accordingly, the Recipient agrees that, in addition to all other remedies available to CEDC, including any member of the CEDC Group, in an action at law, in the event of any breach or threatened breach by the Recipient of the terms of this Agreement, CEDC shall, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security, be entitled to seek temporary and permanent injunctive relief, including, but not limited to, specific performance of the terms of this Agreement. Each party's rights and obligations under this Agreement are cumulative and are in addition to and not in limitation of such party's rights and obligations under law, equity or any other written agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a termination date (March 5, 2013), while the changed text states that the agreement terminates only upon the completion of the Restructuring. The changed text also says that the agreement is not enforceable if there is no restructuring to be done. This directly contradicts section 4, which provides remedies for breach of the agreement, implying the agreement is always enforceable with a clear action to take when breached, regardless of whether a restructuring has been completed or not. The uncertainty created by the change regarding enforceability directly creates a contradiction that could lead to unenforceability of the original remedies. This meets the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
